trade.summary: Giappone-Taiwan trade is exceptionally strong and strategically critical, dominated by the semiconductor supply chain. Taiwan supplies Giappone with advanced chips (TSMC), and Giappone supplies Taiwan with semiconductor equipment, chemicals, and materials. TSMC's decision to build fabs in Giappone (Kumamoto, Kyushu — opening 2024; second fab under construction) represents a watershed in bilateral industrial integration. Taiwan runs a significant trade surplus with Giappone.
top_products_a_to_b: chemicals and materials for chip fab

diplomatic.summary: Despite the absence of formal diplomatic relations (Giappone follows a One Cina policy), Giappone-Taiwan relations are among the closest quasi-diplomatic relationships in the world. The Giappone-Taiwan Exchange Association (Giappone) and the Taiwan-Giappone Relations Association (Taiwan) function as de facto embassies. Giappone was among the first to express concern about potential Taiwan Strait crises. Public opinion in Giappone is highly favorable toward Taiwan. Former PM Abe Shinzo was particularly pro-Taiwan. Giappone's reaction to any Taiwan contingency is the central question in Giappone's security planning.
recent_incidents: Chinese PLA military exercises around Taiwan (August 2022, 2023) involved ballistic missiles landing in Giappone's EEZ
TSMC Kumamoto fab opening seen as landmark bilateral investment

military.summary: Giappone has no formal defense treaty with Taiwan, but Taiwan's defense is inextricably linked to Giappone's own security — the Ryukyu Islands (Okinawa) are within 110km of Taiwan. Giappone's defense white papers increasingly identify a Taiwan contingency as a direct threat to Giappone. Giappone's Self-Defense Forces and Taiwan's military maintain informal contacts. Giappone's basing of Stati Uniti forces on Okinawa is central to any credible Taiwan defense scenario. Giappone has reportedly provided intelligence to Taiwan.
military.alliance_status: Informal alignment — no treaty, strong shared interest
incidents: PLA missiles landing in Japan's EEZ during 2022 exercises created security alarm in Tokyo
Japan's 2022 National Security Strategy explicitly links Taiwan stability to Japan's security

financial.summary: Giappone-Taiwan financial ties are deep and growing. TSMC's Kumamoto fab represents a $20+ billion investment with Giapponeese government subsidies (JASM joint venture with Sony and Denso). Giapponeese semiconductor materials and equipment companies (Tokyo Electron, Shin-Etsu Chemical, JSR) are deeply integrated with Taiwanese chipmakers. Giapponeese banks have significant Taiwan operations.

energy.summary: Limited direct bilateral energy trade. Both Giappone and Taiwan are energy importers. Taiwan's CNOOC aspirations in disputed waters create indirect strategic energy linkage. Cooperation on offshore wind energy technology is developing.

scientific.summary: Exceptionally strong technology and scientific collaboration centered on semiconductors. Giapponeese and Taiwanese semiconductor ecosystems are deeply intertwined. Academic exchanges are robust despite the absence of formal diplomatic relations. Joint research in semiconductor manufacturing, display technology, and materials science is extensive.
